title: Player: 'Heart goes out' to Tiger, but stop driving

title: Puducherry election 2026 updates: Polling ends, voter turnout exceeds 89% in the Union Territory in a historic first

content: “The minor fire was reported at Terminal 1 due to a short circuit at 18:10 p.m. The fire was immediately bought under control by the emergency response teams within minutes. Airport operations remain unaffected,” said CSMIA spokesperson.

content: The latest figures are higher than that of the 2021 Assembly polls which saw a polling percentage of 76%

title: Watch: Sabarimala women entry case: What happened in the Supreme Court?
content: The Sabarimala case is back in the Supreme Court, and this time, the debate has taken a sharp turn. During Wednesday’s hearing, the Centre told the court that some of its most progressive judgments may actually be “not good law.” And at the heart of this argument lies one question: how much power should courts really have over religion? What were the three arguments posed by Solicitor General Tushar Mehta? What is the Sabarimala women entry case all about?

title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE: Several injured in poll-related violence in Assam, seven arrested
content: Three leaders of Assam Jatiya Parishad (AJP) were injured in a clash with BJP supporters at Khowang constituency in Dibrugarh district.

content: The two Quantum Reference Facilities being developed under the State government’s Amaravati Quantum Valley programme are coming up at SRM University in Amaravati and Medha Towers in Gannavaram

content: Over 33,000 temporary registrations issued via VAHAN since March 23, with dealer onboarding and financial integrations streamlining processes, decentralising services and advancing digital governance in transport services

content: Speaking to NDTV, Azar said, "We don't trust the Pakistanis. And I think that, you know, don't get over-enthusiastic to the degree that the Americans trust them. It's a facilitation role, not more than that."

content: Beyond the daily fear of attacks, the most immediate effect of the war has been another upward spiral in the prices of basic goods.

content: The Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojna (MMSY) scheme in Punjab allows families to access cashless treatment of up to Rs 10 lakh a year at both government and empanelled private hospitals.

content: Trump has repeatedly called NATO a "paper tiger" and threatened to withdraw from the 32-member transatlantic alliance in recent weeks, arguing that Washington's European allies have relied on US security guarantees while providing inadequate support

content: West Bengal election 2026: "Despite the immense demand for fish here in Bengal, the state remains far from self-reliant in fish production," Prime Minister Narendra Modi said

content: Netanyahu, the first sitting Israeli prime minister to be charged with a crime, denies charges of bribery, fraud and breach of trust brought in 2019.

content: Death sentences were handed down to two former low-level policemen, Amir Hossain and Sujan Chandra Roy, who are both in court to hear the verdict.

content: This diplomatic movement coincides with Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri's three-day official visit to the United States.

content: Meanwhile, images of Hussain's severed head, lying on the street, apparently captured by onlookers on their phones, have gone viral on social media.

content: A viral video focuses on one particular ritual where nearly 11,000 litres of milk were poured into the Narmada as part of a ceremonial offering.

content: Joining them in the spirit of the day, another bride from northern Wayanad district also made sure to vote first before heading off to her own wedding ceremony.

title: 'Won't Take Eyes Off Putin': UK, Norway Led Op To Deter Russian Submarines
content: Asserting that Russia is the main threat to the UK and its allies, UK Defence Secretary John Healey said, "Putin would want us to be distracted by the Middle East."

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Assaults and confrontations shadow voting in Kottayam, Pathanamthitta
content: Poonjar legislator Sebastian Kulathunkal had a heated confrontation with the police near Koovappally in Kottayam  over the establishment of an LDF booth committee office and  installation of flex boards

title: Kerala Assembly polls 206: Three people cast their votes in Pachakkanam booth inside Periyar Tiger Reserve
content: The booth was specifically set up at the Pachakkanam Anganwadi to accommodate a total of six registered voters: five men and one woman

title: Indian team to visit U.S. this month to discuss trade deal currently stuck in limbo
content: Previously, Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal has indicated that the deal can go forward only if India receives preferential market access as was agreed to in February. The U.S. is yet to finalise its tariffs on other economies

title: Trump tests Nato: US demands pledges to secure Hormuz as Iran chokes strait again
content: The cracks in Nato grew wider after the bloc gave a cold shoulder to the Washington when Trump sought the Cold War-era alliance to step in the war against Iran and use it as a leverage in easing Tehran's grip over Strait of Hormuz that has squeesed oil supply.​​ The strain in the alliance continues even after the last-moment "fragile ceasefire" was reached between US-Iran for two weeks as Europe largely keep itself beyong the ambit of the West Asia war.

title: Shock 40-day blackout: China seals off skies twice the size of Taiwan without warning
content: China has imposed a 40-day ban on civil aviation over a vast airspace near Shanghai, sparking speculation about military activities. The extensive, unexplained restriction, covering over 73,000 sq km, is unusual in its scale and duration, with experts suggesting it's for military exercises. This move is seen as part of China's broader strategy to assert influence in the Indo-Pacific.

title: Schrodinger's ceasefire: Decoding the 'peace treaty' amid US-Israel war with Iran
content: The term Schrödinger’s XYZ has since slipped into the modern lexicon to describe any situation of uncertainty, and it certainly fits the description for the current imbroglio in the Middle East.

title: Andy Jassy's annual shareholders letter has a 'message' for Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang
content: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's shareholder letter signals a significant shift in AI hardware, highlighting Amazon's booming in-house chip business with a $20 billion annual revenue run rate. Jassy indicated potential future sales of these chips to third parties, challenging Nvidia's dominance by offering lower-cost, efficient alternatives for AI workloads.

title: 'Not good for Hinduism': SC flags risks of sect-based temple curbs
content: The Supreme Court expressed concern over sect-based temple restrictions, warning they could harm Hinduism and divide society. A nine-judge bench heard arguments on religious freedom versus state power to open temples, with judges questioning exclusions that might negatively impact the religion and its followers.

title: Umpiring blunder! Finn Allen dismissal sparks row in KKR vs LSG match
content: Umpiring faced backlash in the Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) vs Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) IPL 2026 match after Finn Allen’s controversial catch dismissal by Digvesh Rathi on Thursday. Fans questioned its validity, sparking debate and memes online. Despite the uproar, KKR steadied their innings, but the incident kept umpiring standards under scrutiny.

title: Iran after ceasefire with US: Holding ground in an uncertain pause
content: A fragile two-week ceasefire, brokered by Pakistan, has temporarily halted US-Israel strikes on Iran, shifting focus to fundamental changes and the truce's durability. While Iran has absorbed significant military pressure and its apparatus continues functioning, the conflict has inflicted heavy economic and civilian costs, impacting regional security.

title: What Meta AI head Wang said to those calling Muse Spark AI model 'disappointment'
content: Meta's new AI model, Muse Spark, powering its AI assistant, has drawn criticism for prioritizing benchmarks over real-world utility.  While Meta acknowledges performance gaps, it highlights user satisfaction in areas like visual coding and reasoning.  This initial model, designed for speed and capability, is set to power Meta's platforms soon.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection Day 21: Ranveer Singh starrer dips under Rs 10 crore
content: Aditya Dhar's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its record-breaking spree, crossing Rs 1,000 crore net in India and achieving a worldwide gross of Rs 1,653.67 crore. Despite a slight dip on its 21st day, the film has secured a spot in the top 10 highest-grossing films globally for 2026, marking a significant international triumph for Indian cinema.

title: '6 months left to live': Yuvraj on warning he got before cancer treatment
content: At that time, Yuvraj had just played a big role in India’s 2011 World Cup win, he was Player of the Tournament. During the tournament, he was dealing with fatigue, nausea and discomfort. The illness was not detected then, and he continued to play.

title: Oracle layoffs that shocked employees with 6 am email start to appear in public filings
content: Software giant Oracle is reportedly laying off around 700 workers in California, with job cuts expected by June 1. This move is part of a larger wave of layoffs, potentially impacting thousands, as the company invests heavily in AI infrastructure.  Meanwhile, Oracle faces scrutiny over its H-1B visa filings, sparking debate about American job displacement.

title: Working abroad? How to stay compliant with India's tax residency rules - explained
content: India determines an individual’s tax residency primarily on the basis of physical presence (irrespective of the purpose of stay) within the country.

title: World record! Brazil’s Laura scripts history, becomes first ever to...
content: Brazil’s Laura Cardoso made history by taking a record nine wickets (9/4) in a T20I against Lesotho, the best-ever figures in the format. The 21-year-old bowler surpassed Sonam Yeshey and Rohmalia Rohmalia. Brazil scored 202 before bowling Lesotho out for 13, sealing a massive 189-run win with her unforgettable spell.

title: PAN Card application 2026: Four new forms introduced - which is the right one for you?
content: These forms - Form 93, Form 94, Form 95, and Form 96, have been introduced to suit different categories of applicants, including Indian residents, foreign individuals, as well as domestic and overseas entities.

title: 'Cricket doesn't exist': Ex-B'desh cricketer invites 'all to witness the circus'
content: Bangladesh cricket is facing chaos, with off-field controversies overshadowing the sport. Aftab Ahmed criticised mismanagement, political influence, and media hype, calling it a “circus.” The cricket board’s dissolution and a politically linked new committee added to concerns. He mocked the situation, saying it’s more spectacle than sport, adding: “Cricket doesn’t exist anymore; the circus does.”

title: 'Never added fuel to fire': China rejects claims of military support to Iran
content: China has denied reports of supplying satellite imagery and chip manufacturing equipment to Iran. Beijing stated it has made its own efforts to push for a ceasefire in the region. This comes after Iran and the US agreed to a pause in hostilities. China maintains an objective and impartial stance on the Iran issue, promoting peace talks.

title: No trace of Pawan Khera; Banjara Hills home barricaded
content: Pawan Khera remains untraceable as Assam Police search for him in Hyderabad regarding alleged defamatory remarks against Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife. Khera's Hyderabad residence is under heavy police watch, while he has moved the Telangana High Court seeking anticipatory bail.

title: Narine comes back as KKR asked to bat first against LSG - check full playing XI
content: KKR got a big boost as ace spinner Sunil Narine came back into the side after missing last match against PBKS.

title: Court asks Aditya Dhar, Trimurti Films to mediate Oye Oye song dispute
content: Trimurti Films has filed a copyright infringement lawsuit against Aditya Dhar's production house, Rang De Lal (Oye Oye), over the alleged unauthorized use of the song 'Tirchi Topiwala' from 'Tridev' in 'Dhurandhar 2'. The Delhi High Court has ordered mediation to resolve the dispute, which appears largely financial, concerning royalty and compensation.

title: 'Miller mad?': Jofra Archer's 13-year-old post goes viral after GT beat DC
content: An old post by Jofra Archer went viral after David Miller’s unbeaten 41 nearly won a tense IPL 2026 match. He brought Delhi close but refused a crucial single, leading to a final-ball run-out by Jos Buttler off Prasidh Krishna. Archer’s tweet, “Miller mad? Cricket at 11:30,” perfectly matched the late-night drama.

title: 'Tell cops we are friends': B'luru doc's msg to woman after killing wife
content: A Bengaluru court denied bail to Dr. Mahendra Reddy GS, arrested for allegedly murdering his wife, Kruthika Reddy, by administering an overdose of anaesthesia. Police recovered digital evidence, including incriminating messages, suggesting Reddy planned the murder due to marital discord and his wife's ill health.

title: Watch: China unveils killer canine bots that share brain, primed for urban combat
content: China's military is revolutionizing warfare with advanced canine robots operating in coordinated "wolf packs." These units share intelligence and execute complex urban missions, a significant leap from earlier models. Specialized roles like reconnaissance and offensive operations are assigned, with integration for air-ground missions. This development signals a major evolution in military operations.

title: 'I want this kid out': Yuvraj reveals he asked Bumrah to leave the nets
content: Yuvraj Singh, in an interview, recalled asking a 16-year-old Jasprit Bumrah to stop bowling to him during nets due to his extreme pace.  Yuvraj, then making a comeback after cancer, found the young Bumrah's speed and deceptive bounce incredibly challenging.  He lauded Bumrah as "the best," highlighting his consistent, match-winning performances across all formats.

title: ‘Bomb-making became cottage industry’: PM Modi attacks Mamata Banerjee, TMC at Birbhum rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi fiercely criticized West Bengal's TMC government, accusing it of normalizing violence and turning crude bomb-making into a cottage industry. He promised strict action against infiltrators and land grabbers if the BJP forms the government, highlighting the Bogtui massacre as a blot on humanity and questioning the TMC's respect for tribal communities.

title: Trade deal back in focus: Indian delegation to visit US, says Envoy
content: The United States is set to host an Indian delegation this month, signaling renewed momentum in bilateral trade talks. This follows a productive meeting between US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Indian Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, who is in the US to review trade, defense, and technology ties.

title: 'Riding a fireball': Artemis 2 crew braces for Orion's fiery re-entry
content: Artemis II's crew is preparing for a high-speed re-entry into Earth's atmosphere, a critical and dangerous phase of their mission. The Orion spacecraft's heat shield, which experienced issues on the uncrewed Artemis I mission, is under close watch as it faces extreme temperatures and pressure during its descent.

title: Bengal polls: At Bangladesh border, a fight for next chapter
content: Baharampur's political landscape, shaped by its historical legacy, is witnessing a fierce triangular contest. Once a Congress stronghold, the constituency saw a BJP breakthrough in 2021, followed by a TMC victory in the 2024 Lok Sabha polls. Religious polarization and security concerns are now key drivers, making the upcoming assembly election unpredictable.

title: Nashik: 8 IT company employees allege sexual harassment, 6 held
content: Nashik police have arrested six IT firm employees, including one accused of sexual exploitation under a false marriage promise. Five others face charges of sexual harassment and religious insult against women colleagues over four years. A special investigation team is probing nine registered cases, with an HR official also named as a suspect.

title: IPL 2026: Shubman Gill punished despite GT’s last-ball thriller win over DC
content: Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans clinched a thrilling one-run victory against Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. Despite the dramatic win, Gill was fined Rs 12 lakh for a slow over-rate, marking the team's first offense. The captain's tactical decisions, including a crucial gamble on the final ball, ultimately secured the hard-fought win.

title: Rashmika Mandanna's father gifts her a bungalow on her 30th birthday
content: Actress Rashmika Mandanna received a heartwarming surprise on her 30th birthday from her father, who gifted her a bungalow named 'Serenity' near her hometown. This special occasion followed her recent wedding to Vijay Deverakonda. The actress also celebrated her first birthday post-marriage with a traditional Kodava ceremony in Virajpet, marking a significant new chapter.

title: Not 'chasemaster' Virat Kohli! David Miller, KL Rahul lead this IPL record
content: While RCB star Virat Kohli dominates IPL chase runs, he surprisingly doesn't top the average charts. David Miller leads this elite list with an impressive 49.08 average, followed by KL Rahul and Phil Salt.  Kohli sits tenth, highlighting that sheer volume isn't the only measure of chase prowess in T20 cricket.

title: Hezbollah chief’s nephew Ali Yusuf Harshi killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut
content: Israel announced it killed Ali Yusuf Harshi, nephew of Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem, in a Beirut strike. This occurred despite a ceasefire announcement in a separate conflict. Israeli forces targeted over 100 Hezbollah sites in Beirut and other areas. The strikes followed deadly attacks on commercial and residential areas in Beirut.

title: TCS Q4 FY26 results: 12% rise in net profit; dividend declared
content: For the financial year ended March 31, 2026, TCS said the final dividend will be paid on the third day after the conclusion of its 31st Annual General Meeting.

title: Sensex tanks 900 points: Top reasons for stock market crash
content: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex tanked in trade on Thursday, a day after the stock market saw a record single day gain. While Nifty50 went below 23,800, BSE Sensex was down over 1,000 points intraday.

title: Congress withdraws from Baramati bypoll, 22 independents deny Sunetra Pawar a walkover
content: The Congress has withdrawn its candidate from the Baramati by-election, following an advisory from Sharad Pawar to support NCP candidate Sunetra Pawar. Despite the Congress exit, over 30 independent candidates remain, prompting NCP efforts to persuade them to withdraw before the deadline.

title: ‘Pot of sins now full’: PM Modi attacks TMC’s ‘Nirmam sarkar’ at Asansol rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi asserted that a political change in West Bengal is imminent, with citizens demanding it. Addressing a rally in Asansol, he accused the ruling Trinamool Congress of fostering a 'syndicate raj' controlling the coal and sand sectors, vowing a BJP government would restore industrial strength and end mafia rule.

title: Was Lebanon part of ceasefire deal or not? How Pakistan may have muddled US-Iran truce
content: A ceasefire between the US and Iran is shrouded in confusion, with sharp disagreements over its scope, particularly regarding Lebanon. While the US insists the truce is limited to Iran, Pakistan and Iran suggest a broader agreement. This ambiguity, coupled with continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon and differing US negotiation frameworks, threatens the fragile peace ahead of crucial talks.

title: ‘Intent can’t be faulted, but…’: Gavaskar defends Miller after DC’s one-run heartbreak
content: Sunil Gavaskar defended David Miller's intent in Delhi Capitals' narrow IPL loss, emphasising execution challenges in high-pressure moments. He highlighted Ravi Shastri's 1986 tied Test heroics as an example of crucial game awareness. Meanwhile, Rashid Khan's stellar bowling secured Gujarat Titans' first win, though Kevin Pietersen noted overall bowling concerns.

title: Rankings: Profiling more than 60 wide receivers
content: Mike Clay is revealing his fantasy football rookie rankings at each key offensive position . the rankings do not include rookies because we are unsure of their landing spots . all references to player ages are as of the season opener on sept. 9, 2026 .

title: Coaches, GMs weigh in on SEC's top newcomers
content: new faces will be announced all over the SEC in 2026 . coaches, general managers and personnel staffers selected one newcomer for each SEC team with a path to immediate contribution . RB Ezavier and RB ezavier are among the players to add to the roster .

title: Meet the newest source for steals in Chicago
content: the fantasy baseball buzz file aims to provide fantasy managers with the intel they need as news breaks around MLB . the six-game slate kicks off at 12:10 p.m. ET with the Miami Marlins hosting the Cincinnati Reds .

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25: Ranking the best teams for next season, now
content: the 2026-27 college basketball season is expected to take place in 2026 . we're already rolling right into the "offseason," which mostly involves rampant roster changes for the next six weeks . in the past, we've had pretty hard-and-fast house rules regarding the Way-Too-Early Top 25 .

title: Pochettino's to-do list: What the USMNT must figure out before World Cup
content: Mauricio Pochettino will have a pre-World Cup camp to get his side ready for competition . he'll have to lock in his choices on which players will join him in the world cup cauldron . the men's national team are ranked outside the top 20 contenders to win the 2026 world cup .

title: 'I'm going to put it in my house in a safe': How the Masters lawn gnome became so popular that it might be discontinued
content: Ryan Carey is considered the world's most accomplished golf memorabilia expert . the gnome has been a kitschy decorative item for centuries . it's the only item available on site that is limited to one purchase per patron .

title: Roman Reigns' Top 10 WrestleMania main events: Which match finished No. 1?
content: Roman Reigns will challenge CM Punk at WrestleMania 42 Night 2 on April 18 at Allegiant Stadium in Las Vegas for the world heavyweight championship in his 11th main event . he defeated Brock Lesnar at the "Winner Take All" main event between lesnar and thera .

title: Stick or sack? Slot's Liverpool future dominates UCL talking points

title: Masters tiers: Ranking all 91 players in the field
content: for the first time in his career, Rory McIlroy will drive down Magnolia Lane this week as a Masters champion . a year ago, he made a 4-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole to defeat england's Justin Rose on the first hole of a sudden-death playoff . with his long-awaited Masters victory, Mcilroy became only the sixth golfer to complete the career Grand Slam .

title: WNBA mock draft: UConn's Fudd drops to No. 3 pick
content: Olivia Miles drops 28 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists as TCU puts away Virginia 79-69 . the college players available in Monday's draft had one last audition in the tournament . in our latest WNBA mock draft, for example, Miles projected as a lottery pick last season when she was eligible age-wise .

title: Back to the future: Repicking the 2016 NHL draft
content: it has been almost 10 years since the 2016 NHL draft was held in Buffalo . the team reshuffled the picks with hindsight, taking into account what teams were looking for at the time and how these players have performed for the past decade . title: NBA teams all want to play faster -- but it might be making them worse
content: a record 18 teams are averaging at least 100 possessions per game . the new york Knicks wanted to play faster this season . they wanted to trade the dance team, concession workers and anyone besides Jalen Brunson to the Bucks .

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: the women's college basketball transfer portal opened Monday with a tidal wave of activity . more than 1,300 players are looking for new homes . the portal closes April 20 but players' decisions for their next school can still come after that .

title: Sights and sounds from the first round of the Masters
content: Bubba Watson addresses Tiger Woods' DUI charge ahead of The Masters. (0:47) The 90th edition of the Masters Tournament began on Thursday, kicking off the first golf major of the year. It is one of the most iconic events in sports, and there was, as always, no shortage of iconic scenery at Augusta National as the tournament began. From the course to the concessions, all the classic staples were there to accompany the on-course play. Here are all the top sights and sounds from the first round of the Masters. title: Air India's Bengaluru-bound flight returns safely to Mumbai after engine stall
content:
.

NEW DELHI: An Air India Airbus A320 (VT-TNK) operating from Mumbai to Bengaluru on Thursday returned safely to the origin shortly after take off after one of its engines stalled. The pilots declared PAN PAN to seek a priority return to Mumbai. Some passengers reportedly saw sparks from the engine during take off and heard a sound. An Air India spokesperson said: "Flight AI2812 from Mumbai to Bengaluru on April 9 returned to Mumbai shortly after take‑off as a precaution due to a suspected technical issue, in accordance with standard operating procedures. The aircraft landed safely and is undergoing detailed technical inspections in line with Air India’s safety standards." "We regret the inconvenience caused to our passengers. Our Mumbai airport teams provided immediate assistance, and an alternative aircraft was arranged to operate the flight. The safety and wellbeing of our passengers and crew remain Air India’s top priority," the spokesperson added.

End of Article
